economic_notes: Xaxli'p Development Corporation (wholly-owned business entity accounted for by modified equity method) had total revenue of $2,048,217 and total expenses of $2,048,172 for year ended March 31, 2021. COVID-19 impacted operations with increased government funding from ISC and FNHA, additional social assistance, cleaning costs, and cancellation of in-person events.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 but audit was signed August 30, 2022 — over a year delay. Three fully consolidated subsidiaries: Tmicw Land Holdings Ltd., Xaxli'p Community Forest Corporation, and Fountain Valley Services Society. One GBE (Xaxli'p Development Corporation) accounted for by modified equity method with investment value of $2,041,273. Funds held in Ottawa Trust of $481,896 included as financial asset. expenses_infrastructure includes Public Works ($323,964), Ministry of Transportation and Infrastructure Slide Stabilization Project ($298,519), and Resources ($428,493) was classified separately under Resources segment; expenses_enterprises reflects Economic Development only. Large deferred revenue increase of $1,574,621 during year. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($3,775,056) + CMHC ($168,248) + FNHA ($1,395,152) + BC Gaming ($528,980) + FNESC ($107,170) + Province of BC ($81,555) + St'at'imc Trust ($75,696) = $6,131,857; osr = $7,772,853 - $6,131,857 = $1,640,996. Note: Aboriginal Skills and Employment Training Strategy and Work Opportunity Program had $0 in 2021 (prior year only).
